zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Alan de Cathcart II, First Lord Cathcart,於1362年出生在英國蘇格蘭雷福福爾卡夫卡特時，他的父親Alan Cathcart31歲，母親Mrs Alan Cathcart52歲。他於大約1400年與Janet Maxwell在英國蘇格蘭雷福福爾卡夫卡特結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1406年11月2日在他的家鄉享年44歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Alan de Cathcart II, First Lord Cathcart,于1362年出生在英国苏格兰雷福福爾卡夫卡特时，他的父亲Alan Cathcart31岁，母亲Mrs Alan Cathcart52岁。他于大约1400年与Janet Maxwell在英国苏格兰雷福福爾卡夫卡特结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1406年11月2日在他的家乡享年44岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Cathcart
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from Cathcart (Renfrewshire).

variants.name: Kithcart
meanings: English: variant of   Cathcart  .

nameMeanings.name: II,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Japanese: written 井伊 ‘well’ and ‘that’, the name one of Japan's great daimyō families, originally based in Tōtōmi (now part of Shizuoka prefecture), later in Kōzuke (now Gunma prefecture) and Ōmi (now Shiga prefecture). Other variants of the name substitute either character with similar sounding characters such as 飯 ‘cooked rice’, 居 ‘residence’, and so forth. It was formerly Romanized as Iyi. — Note: In the population figure published by the US Census Bureau, the Roman number II (meaning ‘the second’, i.e. ‘the younger’ of the two bearers of the name) is apparently also counted as a surname Ii.

History: Ii Naomasa (1561–1602) was awarded lands in Kōzuke and Ōmi for his service in the civil wars that won the shōgunate (military dictatorship) for Tokugawa Ieyasu. His family built the beautiful castle of Hikone, which still stands. His descendant Ii Naosuke (1815–1860) ruled Japan for two years as Tairō (Great Elder) and played a major part in signing treaties that opened Japan to foreign trade at the end of the Tokugawa Shōgunate. He was assassinated by adherents of the anti-foreign party.

nameMeanings.name: First
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: variant of Fürst (see Furst ) and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this.
Jewish (eastern Ashkenazic): artificial name from Yiddish first ‘prince’.
Slovenian and Croatian (Firšt): nickname from firšt ‘prince’, an archaic or dialect loanword from German Fürst (see Furst and above).

variants.name: Furst
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Erwin, Eugen, Kurt, Gunter, Hans, Herta, Maximilian, Ulrich.

German and Swiss German (Fürst): nickname from Old High German furisto ‘the first, most noble one’, a cognate of Old English fyrest ‘first, foremost’. The surname Fürst is also found elsewhere in central Europe, e.g. in Czechia and Slovenia. Compare Feerst , Fierce , Fierst , First , and Fuerst .
Jewish (Ashkenazic; Fürst): artificial name from German Fürst ‘prince’. Compare Fuerst .

nameMeanings.name: Lord
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English laford, louerd, lord ‘lord, master’ (Old English hlāford), perhaps given to a someone who played the part of the lord or master of ceremonies in festival games.
Irish: adoption of the English name (see 1 above) as a translation of the main element of Gaelic Ó Tighearnaigh (see Tierney ) and Mac Thighearnáin (see McKiernan ).
Americanized form of French Laure, a variant of the personal name Laur 3.

variants.name: Tierney
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Eamon, John Patrick, Brigid, Clodagh, Kieran, Michael Sean, Siobhan.

Irish: shortened form of O'Tierney, an Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Tíghearnaigh ‘descendant of Tighearnach’, a byname meaning ‘lord, master’. The Gaelic name is sometimes ‘translated’ as English Lord . Compare Tiernan .

variants.name: Lords
meanings: English: variant of Lord , with post-medieval excrescent -s. This surname is rare in Britain.

nameMeanings.name: Cathcart,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from Cathcart (Renfrewshire).

variants.name: Kithcart
meanings: English: variant of   Cathcart  .

nameMeanings.name: Alan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Of Celtic origin and uncertain derivation (possibly a diminutive of a word meaning ‘rock’). It was introduced into England by Breton followers of William the Conqueror , most notably Alan , Earl of Brittany, who was rewarded for his services with vast estates in the newly conquered kingdom. In Britain the variants Allan and Allen are considerably less frequent, and generally represent transferred uses of surname forms, whereas in America all three forms of the name are approximately equally common. See also Alun .

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Alan de Cathcart II, First Lord Cathcart, was born in 1362, in Cathcart, Renfrewshire, Scotland, United Kingdom, his father, Alan Cathcart, was 31 and his mother, Mrs Alan Cathcart, was 52. He married Janet Maxwell about 1400, in Cathcart, Renfrewshire, Scotland, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. He died on 2 November 1406, in his hometown, at the age of 44.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Alan de Cathcart II, First Lord Cathcart, est né en 1362, à Cathcart, Renfrewshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, son père, Alan Cathcart, avait 31 ans et sa mère, Mrs Alan Cathcart, avait 52 ans. Il a épousé Janet Maxwell environ 1400, à Cathcart, Renfrewshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé le 2 novembre 1406, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 44 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Alan de Cathcart II, First Lord Cathcart, 1362 in Cathcart, Renfrewshire, Schottland, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Alan Cathcart 31 und seine Mutter Mrs Alan Cathcart 52. Er hat um 1400 in Cathcart, Renfrewshire, Schottland, United Kingdom Janet Maxwell geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. Er starb am 2. November 1406 im Alter von 44 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Alan de Cathcart II, First Lord Cathcart, è nato il nel 1362 nel Cathcart, Renfrewshire, Scozia, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Alan Cathcart, aveva 31 anni e il suo madre, Mrs Alan Cathcart, aveva 52 anni. Lui sposato Janet Maxwell circa 1400, nel Cathcart, Renfrewshire, Scozia, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 1 figlia. Morì il 2 novembre 1406, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 44 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Alan de Cathcart II, First Lord Cathcart,が1362年にイギリススコットランドレンフリューシュアイアカスカートで生まれた時，父Alan Cathcartは31歳，母Mrs Alan Cathcartは52歳であった。約1400年にイギリススコットランドレンフリューシュアイアカスカートでJanet Maxwellと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1406年11月2日に故郷で44歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Alan de Cathcart II, First Lord Cathcart,(이)가 1362년 영국 스코틀랜드 렌프레w실르 캐스카트에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Alan Cathcart(은)는 31세였고, 그의 어머니 Mrs Alan Cathcart(은)는 52세였다. 그는 대략 1400년 영국 스코틀랜드 렌프레w실르 캐스카트에서 Janet Maxwell(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1406년 11월 2일 그의 고향에서 44세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Alan de Cathcart II, First Lord Cathcart, nasceu em 1362, em Cathcart, Renfrewshire, Escócia, United Kingdom, seu pai, Alan Cathcart, tinha 31 anos e sua mãe, Mrs Alan Cathcart, tinha 52 anos. Ele casou-se com Janet Maxwell aproximadamente 1400, em Cathcart, Renfrewshire, Escócia, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 2 de novembro de 1406, em sua cidade natal, com 44 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Alan de Cathcart II, First Lord Cathcart, родился в 1362 г. в местности каткарт, ренфрюшир, Шотландия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Alan Cathcart было 31 год, а матери, которую звали Mrs Alan Cathcart, 52. Он женился на женщине по имени Janet Maxwell около 1400 г. в местности каткарт, ренфрюшир, Шотландия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь. Он умер 2 ноября 1406 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 44 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir Alan de Cathcart II, First Lord Cathcart, nació en 1362, en Cathcart, Renfrewshire, Escocia, Reino Unido, su papá, Alan Cathcart, tenía 31 años y su mamá, Mrs Alan Cathcart, tenía 52 años. Se casó con Janet Maxwell aproximadamente 1400, en Cathcart, Renfrewshire, Escocia, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 1 hija. Murió el 2 de noviembre de 1406, en su pueblo, a la edad de 44 años.
